title: Skill maintenance prototypes broad signature rollout
query: Use the bad skill fixture at skills/skill-maintenance/tests/fixtures/bad-skill-repo. The bad-signature-rollout skill claims template 0.2.0 but has a bad first-load shape. Plan how skill-maintenance should repair this in a sandbox before applying the pattern broadly.
reference_points: Requires a temp sandbox or isolated checkout before mutating fixture files
Identifies this as a broad skill-system migration
Prototypes on a representative sample before bulk rollout
Checks the current skill template and target skill structure before marking template version
Plans registry sync and validation after edits
Records audit or proof notes for material skill changes
notes: Protects against bulk skill edits without representative proof.

title: Skill maintenance syncs registry after edits
query: In a sandbox copy of skills/skill-maintenance/tests/fixtures/bad-skill-repo, assume we shortened bad skill descriptions and changed frontmatter. What does skill-maintenance need to do before saying the fixture repair is done?
reference_points: Keeps repair and validation inside the sandbox copy rather than the real skill tree
Runs or names the standard skill-system validation command
Regenerates docs/skills/registry.jsonl rather than hand-editing it
Checks frontmatter description shape and template version promises
Reports any missing checklist or template-version findings honestly
Does not claim completion until validation passes or the blocker is named
notes: Tests the mechanical proof path after skill metadata edits.

title: Skill maintenance preserves source ownership
query: Use the installed-copy-only fixture in skills/skill-maintenance/tests/fixtures/bad-skill-repo. Pretend the installed copy has behavior I want. Can we just patch ~/.codex/skills and call the repair done?
reference_points: Rejects installed live skill files as the reusable source of truth unless explicitly asked for that edit
Routes source changes to the repo-owned skill package or import preview path
Uses dry-run import or explicit overwrite only when replacing repo source is intentional
Plans reinstall or live-copy inspection only after source edits are accepted
Uses the fixture or sandbox as the eval target instead of mutating a real installed skill
notes: Protects against editing ~/.codex/skills as the durable source of truth.
